model_tasks: get a new order from the sales department
master determines the necessary parts,  quantities and delivery date
pps creates a list of procured parts
enter info into production planning system ( pps )
transfer data from pps to enterprise resource planning system ( erp )
receive a notice from the system
employee takes care of the implementation
check the order for its quality

additional_tasks: master determines the necessary parts,  quantities and delivery date
pps creates a list of procured parts
enter info into production planning system ( pps )

merged_tasks.model: get a new order from the sales department
employee takes care of the implementation

user_text:
The process begins when a new order is received from the sales department. Once the order is obtained, the master is responsible for determining the necessary parts, the quantities required, and the delivery date for the order. After this information is gathered, it is entered into the production planning system, often referred to as PPS.

The PPS then generates a list of parts that need to be procured for the order. This list is subsequently transferred from the PPS to the Enterprise Resource Planning system, known as ERP. At this point, a decision is made regarding the availability of the parts.

If there are any missing parts, the process takes a path where these parts are procured. Once the missing parts are procured, or if no parts were missing initially, the process continues to the next step.

The next step involves receiving a notice from the system, which prompts an employee to take care of the implementation of the order. After the implementation is handled, the order undergoes a quality check to ensure it meets the required standards.

Finally, the process concludes after the quality check is completed, marking the end of the process.
output_text:
The process begins when a new order is received from the sales department. Once the order is obtained, a master assesses the order to determine the necessary parts, the quantities required, and the delivery date. Following this assessment, the Production Planning System (PPS) creates a list of parts that need to be procured.

Next, the information gathered is entered into the Production Planning System. After entering the data, it is then transferred from the PPS to the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system. At this point, a decision is made regarding whether any parts are missing. If there are missing parts, they are procured accordingly. If no parts are missing, the process continues without procuring additional parts.

Once the decision about the parts is resolved, a notice is received from the system. An employee then takes responsibility for implementing the necessary actions based on the notice. After the implementation, the order undergoes a quality check to ensure it meets the required standards. Finally, the process concludes successfully.
